lyrics:
Ladies and gentlemen
Citizens of the groove
When the news gets strange
And the bills get bigger
And your thinker won’t quit stinkin’
There is only one thing left to do
Funk it

Alarm clock screaming like the world’s on fire
Everybody selling doom for a dollar higher
Talking heads twitching on a flat-screen wall
Nobody knows nothing but they know it all

Got a pocket full of worry and a head full of noise
Every false prophet got a brand-new toy
Left shoe missing and the rent’s due too
Whole wide world gone cuckoo

You can clench your fist
You can bite your lip
You can try to make sense of a sinking ship
Or you can shake that doubt till it falls off your bones
Let the rhythm take over and carry you home

Funk it
When the whole thing’s shot
Funk it
Give it all you got
Funk it
When the truth gets thin
Throw your hands up high and let the funk roll in
Funk it
When the wires all cross
Funk it
When you can’t stop the loss
Funk it
When it’s all too much
Put some grease on your soul and strut

Boss man smiling with a snake oil grin
Says work twice harder just to barely win
Politicians dancing with a forked-up tongue
Singing same old song while they rob the young

Your phone keeps buzzing like a bad tattoo
Everybody wants a little piece of you
Mind gone sideways, heart’s worn thin
That’s when the real ones funk within

When the facts all blur
And the fear runs thick
When the whole damn system makes you feel seasick
Don’t let the madness make a monument of you
Drop it in the one and do what the people do

Funk it
When the whole thing’s shot
Funk it
Give it all you got
Funk it
When the truth gets thin
Throw your hands up high and let the funk roll in
Funk it
When the wires all cross
Funk it
When you can’t stop the loss
Funk it
When it’s all too much
Put some grease on your soul and strut

Funk it to the left
Funk it to the right
Funk it in the morning
Funk it all night
Funk it for the broke
Funk it for the beat
Funk it for the people trying not to be beat

When the map ain’t true
And the road’s all bent
When your last good dime’s already been spent
When your brain says no but your hips say yes
That’s the spirit, baby
Now funk that mess

Funk it
When the world’s insane
Funk it
Dance away the pain
Funk it
Let the bad vibes spin
We ain’t going down, we’re funking through again
Funk it
When the room gets dark
Funk it
Be the lightning spark
Funk it
When it all goes nuts
We ain’t out of hope till the groove gives up

You can’t fix stupid
You can’t preach peace to a chainsaw
You can’t make a straight line out of a crooked age
But you can still move
You can still laugh
You can still love
You can still funk it
persona:
## PCBender

Rugged, wise, and deeply entrenched in the art of music-making, PCBender is more than just a musician—he’s a sonic craftsman. A guitar-slinging songwriter, producer, and storyteller, he embodies the soul of analog warmth, weaving together rich textures of classic rock, blues, and experimental soundscapes with a modern edge.

With a presence that carries the mystique of a seasoned explorer, PCBender is the Indiana Jones of music production—digging deep into the essence of sound, unearthing raw emotion, and shaping it into songs that resonate beyond time. His music is a fusion of grit and refinement, where worn-in guitar tones meet polished production, and every note feels like it was carved from experience.

A producer at heart, he doesn’t just create songs—he builds them. Each track is an expedition through layers of melody, distortion, and lyrical depth, reflecting both his technical expertise and his philosopher’s approach to songwriting. Whether delivering anthemic riffs, cinematic storytelling, or stripped-down acoustic wisdom, PCBender brings an authenticity that makes listeners feel like they’re sitting in the studio with him, watching music come to life.

As a key figure in Maricopa Records, PCBender bridges the gap between analog and AI-driven creativity, proving that the heart of a song is never about how it’s made, but why.

For those who seek music with depth, texture, and soul—PCBender is your guide.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/0aivrU155laeOcIoT4AhPo?si=pYdQp1J_S4y0vSFA4itgUg)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/pcbender/1793178641)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCvNXkiIHQJM-uY4MT3gmKDw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/PCBender.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track opens with a gritty, spoken word intro before exploding into a high-energy funk groove with a defiant and celebratory atmosphere. The energy is insistent and propulsive, carried by a tight rhythm section, punchy horns, and soulful call-and-response vocals that create an infectious, danceable feel. The production is modern and powerful, creating a dense yet spacious mix where the foundational bass and drums sit tightly together while allowing the vocal and instrumental layers to cut through with clarity. A defining quality is the track's central hook and title, which serves as a recurring, cathartic release and a powerful response to the lyrical frustrations expressed in the verses.

review.review_text: PCBender opens with a spoken-word address to "citizens of the groove" before the rhythm section drops and the whole thing locks into a G-major strut at a crisp 108 BPM that doesn't let up for nearly four minutes. The hook is blunt and perfectly built: "put some grease on your soul and strut" lands every time because the track earns it, with punchy horns and a bass that sits deep in the pocket making the release feel physical rather than rhetorical. Lyrically it's sharp where a lot of protest-funk goes soft, trading in specific images like "talking heads twitching on a flat-screen wall" and "your phone keeps buzzing like a bad tattoo" rather than vague grievance. It sits comfortably in the company of James Brown's more pissed-off moments, and the final spoken-word coda, "you can't make a straight line out of a crooked age, but you can still funk it," is the kind of closer that sticks.
anchors_used: hook lyric: put some grease on your soul and strut
lyric: talking heads twitching on a flat-screen wall
lyric: your phone keeps buzzing like a bad tattoo
